name: Dennis' Dinner Crepes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Barley flour or corn flour
1/4 cup Safflower oil; plus extra for pan
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: St. Louis Post-Dispatch, 3/10/97
1. In a large bowl, combine rice flour, barley flour, arrowroot, tapioca flour, and salt.
2. Gradually whisk in milk, then add 1/4 cup oil.
3. Heat a 10-inch nonstick skillet or crepe pan over medium-high heat; lightly brush the surface with oil.
4. Pour about 1/3 cup batter into the pan for each crepe. Tilt the pan to swirl the batter evenly over the surface, then pour any excess batter back into the bowl.
5. Cook each crepe for 1 minute, then flip and cook an additional 30 seconds or until lightly browned.
6. Yield: About 12 crepes.
7. Note: Cooked crepes can be stacked between layers of paper towels, then wrapped in plastic wrap and refrigerated or frozen.
